title: Votive Seal
description: A PQC-signed provenance record attesting the identity of a materialized image (NixOS closure, OCI layer, or unikernel). The deploy plane (preceptord) only admits images whose closure measurement matches a valid votive seal. See RFC 0007 §5.

kind.description: Discriminator constant. Always 'votive-seal'. Guards against applying the admission check to unrelated JSON documents.

version.description: Schema version of this seal document. Follows SemVer; the current generation is '1'.

closure_hash.description: SHA-256 hex digest of the materialized NixOS closure (or OCI manifest digest, or unikernel measurement). 64 lowercase hex characters. Produced by 'nix path-info --hash', 'sha256sum', or an equivalent hermetic content-hash. The deploy plane compares this to the live store path hash before admitting the image.

topology_epoch.description: The topology epoch under which this seal was issued (RFC 0005 §2.4). The deploy plane rejects seals from a stale epoch. Epoch 0 is reserved for bootstrapping; production seals start at epoch 1.

membrane.description: The declared Vaked membrane path this closure implements, in 'kind/name' notation (e.g. 'network/agent-egress', 'filesystem/artifacts'). Matched against the deploy plane's membrane-to-closure allowlist.

signed_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp (UTC, with 'Z' suffix) at which the seal was signed. Informational only — the deploy plane checks topology_epoch and closure_hash, not wall-clock time.

signature.description: Cryptographic signature over the canonical JSON of this document (with 'signature' excluded). In production this is ML-DSA-65 (FIPS 204 / CRYSTALS-Dilithium3 — the PQC lattice-based scheme mandated by RFC 0007 §3). During the spike, HMAC-SHA256 is used as a placeholder.

algorithm.description: Signature algorithm identifier. 'ml-dsa-65' is FIPS 204 ML-DSA (Dilithium3) — requires liboqs or a Zig ML-DSA implementation. 'hmac-sha256-placeholder' is a spike-only stand-in that MUST NOT be used in production (see 'placeholder' field).

value.description: Base64-encoded signature bytes. For 'ml-dsa-65': the full 3309-byte Dilithium3 signature. For 'hmac-sha256-placeholder': base64(HMAC-SHA256(key, canonical_payload)).

public_key.description: Optional base64-encoded public key material. For 'ml-dsa-65': the 1952-byte Dilithium3 public key (included so offline verifiers can check without a key store). For 'hmac-sha256-placeholder': omitted (symmetric key is not shipped in the seal).

placeholder.description: Present and true ONLY when algorithm is 'hmac-sha256-placeholder'. Signals to the admission controller that this is a spike seal and must not be used in a production trust policy. The real implementation requires liboqs (Python oqs bindings) or the Zig ML-DSA-65 implementation referenced in RFC 0007 §6.
